creator: bill@duchess.wagill.com
text:
It is a Mitsumi internal CD-ROM drive model FX001D, double speed; with an ISA
interface card; using I/O port address 0x300 (default), IRQ 10 (default), and
DRQ 5 (default) (although FreeBSD does not seem to reference the DRQ). 

The CD-ROM is found during the probe: 

	mcd0: type Mitsumi FX001D, version info:  D 2
	mcd0 at 0x300-0x303 irq 10 on isa

The CD-ROM disc is in the drive before the boot-up is started.

This same drive and hardware configuration worked just fine when 
installing FreeBSD 2.1 from the Walnut Creek CD-ROM.  I have made no 
hardware changes since then.

Any help is greatly appreciates; I am eager to come up on 2.1.5.

Thank you.

- Bill.


On Mon, 19 Aug 1996, Jordan K. Hubbard wrote:

> Unfortunately, this does not give me anywhere near enough information
> to help you.  What model of Mitsumi?  How's it hooked up?
> 
> 					Jordan

> > 	I have a Mistumi CD-ROM drive (ISA), and this same CD-ROM drive worked 
> just
